title: Configuration Drift vs CIS Hardening Benchmark on Windows CDE Members

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 2.2.3 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 2.2.3 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.9: Configuration Drift vs CIS Hardening Benchmark on Windows CDE Members.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.9' running on index=vm sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:compliance","qualys:policy") — plugin_name, host_fqdn, compliance_status, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964)
spl:
index=vm sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:compliance","qualys:policy") earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_windows_cde_assets.csv fqdn AS host_fqdn OUTPUT cde_member
| where cde_member="true"
| eval fail=if(match(compliance_status,"(?i)failed|error|critical"),1,0)
| stats sum(fail) as failed_checks, count as total_checks by host_fqdn
| eval fail_pct=if(total_checks>0, round(100*failed_checks/total_checks,2), null())
| where fail_pct>5
| sort - fail_pct
description: Aggregates configuration assessment findings against a documented hardening standard so you can show continuous alignment with industry-accepted secure configurations for in-scope Windows systems.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes vm and sourcetypes qualys:policy, tenable:sc:compliance. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Map scanner asset names to PCI inventory FQDNs. (2) Scope policies to CIS Windows Server benchmarks used in your standard. (3) Track `fail_pct` trend weekly. (4) Attach exports to secure configuration evidence repository. Indexes required: `index=vm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=qualys:policy`, `sourcetype=tenable:sc:compliance`; Lookups: `pci_windows_cde_assets`, `pci_windows_cde_assets.csv`.
visualization: Bar chart (fail_pct by host), heatmap (plugin families), single value (mean fail_pct across CDE).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t dc(Vulnerabilities.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Scan failures during legacy system lifecycle, documented compensating controls, or planned upgrade windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vm` `sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:compliance","qualys:policy")` — `plugin_name`, `host_fqdn`, `compliance_status`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map scanner asset names to PCI inventory FQDNs. (2) Scope policies to CIS Windows Server benchmarks used in your standard. (3) Track `fail_pct` trend weekly. (4) Attach exports to secure configuration evidence repository.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vm sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:compliance","qualys:policy") earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_windows_cde_assets.csv fqdn AS host_fqdn OUTPUT cde_member
| where cde_member="true"
| eval fail=if(match(compliance_status,"(?i)failed|error|critical"),1,0)
| stats sum(fail) as failed_checks, count as total_checks by host_fqdn
| eval fail_pct=if(total_checks>0, round(100*failed_checks/total_checks,2), null())
| where fail_pct>5
| sort - fail_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Configuration Drift vs CIS Hardening Benchmark on Windows CDE Members (PCI DSS Req 2.2.1, 2.2.3)** — Aggregates configuration assessment findings against a documented hardening standard so you can show continuous alignment with industry-accepted secure configurations for in-scope Windows systems.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:compliance","qualys:policy")` — `plugin_name`, `host_fqdn`, `compliance_status`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vm.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vm, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where cde_member="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fail** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host_fqdn** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fail_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where fail_pct>5` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(Vulnerabilities.cvss) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Configuration Drift vs CIS Hardening Benchmark on Windows CDE Members (PCI DSS Req 2.2.1, 2.2.3)** — Aggregates configuration assessment findings against a documented hardening standard so you can show continuous alignment with industry-accepted secure configurations for in-scope Windows systems.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:compliance","qualys:policy")` — `plugin_name`, `host_fqdn`, `compliance_status`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (fail_pct by host), heatmap (plugin families), single value (mean fail_pct across CDE).
grandmaExplanation: We aggregates configuration assessment findings against a documented hardening standard so you can show continuous alignment with industry-accepted secure configurations for in-scope Windows systems. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Vulnerabilities (CIM data model).
